meta.description: Seed nodes for the Lexy AI LMS causal concept graph. Each entry maps to one Qdrant point in lexy_causal_nodes. Vectors are computed at ingest via fingerprint_csv_capabilities or server-side embedding. Node IDs without a domain suffix are multi-domain nodes.

domain_context.lms: Percentage of active learners who have completed all assigned mandatory training within the policy window. Primary audit-facing terminal metric. All Shapley attribution for LMS compliance gaps is computed against this node.
domain_context.hr: Workforce compliance posture indicator — used as a confounder signal in attrition and workforce-risk models.

nodes.description: Mandatory training completion rate vs organisational policy target. Current vs target gap drives gap_analysis and compliance_gap_close intents.

domain_context.lms: Percentage of employees current on required certifications (not expired, not overdue). Terminal for certification_management intents and cert-bounded compliance gap analysis.

nodes.description: Ratio of employees holding current (non-expired) certifications to total employees required to hold them.

domain_context.lms: Compliance with continuing education credit requirements. Different from cert_compliance_rate — measures hours/credits accumulated rather than binary certification status.

nodes.description: Percentage of learners meeting their annual or cycle CE credit hour requirements.

domain_context.lms: Ratio of assessment attempts resulting in a passing score. Terminal for training_effectiveness intents. High in_degree from score_average, score_distribution, and eval_completion_rate.

nodes.description: Percentage of assessment attempts achieving the passing threshold. Lagging effectiveness outcome.

domain_context.lms: Total training spend divided by learners served. Terminal for training_roi_analysis. Driven by vendor_cost_per_hour, cancellation losses, and completion_count_total.

nodes.description: Blended average cost of delivering training per active learner across all modalities.

domain_context.lms: Estimated value delivered per dollar of training spend. Latent — requires cost-side and outcome-side data pair. Observable only when both training_cost and a measurable outcome metric (compliance rate improvement, audit findings reduction) are connected.

nodes.description: Ratio of measurable training outcomes value to total training cost. Requires cost+outcome data pair.

domain_context.lms: Percentage of role-required skills evidenced by completed training. Terminal for skill_gap_analysis intents. Low match rates indicate systemic curriculum gaps rather than individual learner failures.

nodes.description: Ratio of role-required skill competencies evidenced by completed training to total required competencies.

domain_context.lms: Weekly count of learner logins. STABLE trend rules out disengagement as root cause in compliance gaps — used to distinguish assignment overload from motivational failure. DECLINING trend triggers anomaly_detection and precedes completion drops by ~21 days.
domain_context.hr: Platform access regularity as proxy for workforce engagement. Declining trend correlates with early attrition signals in HR models with ~3 week lag.

nodes.description: Aggregate weekly login count across active learners. Leading engagement signal. Stable = disengagement ruled out; declining = anomaly threshold.

domain_context.lms: Volume and timing of mandatory training assignments distributed per period. Root cause of overdue_count spikes — batch assignment events create immediate overdue inventory. The 42d full causal chain to compliance_rate means batch events must be identified before the 30d audit window closes.

nodes.description: Count and temporal distribution of compliance training assignments. Batch assignment spikes are the primary root cause of overdue surges.

domain_context.lms: Denominator for all rate-based metrics. Confoundes department-level comparisons — size-adjustment required when comparing completion rates across cohorts of different size.
domain_context.hr: Active workforce headcount as sourced from HR system of record. Used for denominator reconciliation.

nodes.description: Total count of active employees in the learner denominator. Confounder for department-level rate comparisons.

domain_context.lms: Number of available qualified ILT instructors. Root constraint on ILT delivery capacity. Insufficient instructor count → waitlist growth → attendance reduction → cert delays. Not applicable for web-based training analysis.

nodes.description: Count of qualified and available ILT instructors. Primary supply-side root for ILT capacity chain.

domain_context.lms: Unit cost of externally sourced training content or delivery per hour. Root driver of training_cost_total in vendor-heavy programmes. Primary lever for cost optimisation.

nodes.description: Average cost per training-hour charged by external vendors. Root node in cost chain.

domain_context.lms: Proportion of learning activity that is self-initiated vs assigned. High ratio correlates with higher score_average and lower fail_count. Exogenous — reflects learner motivation posture rather than admin actions.

nodes.description: Fraction of total learning events initiated by the learner without an explicit assignment. Leading indicator for assessment performance.

domain_context.lms: Total budget allocated and spent on learning programmes in the period. Used as cost-side input for training_roi_analysis. Separate from training_cost_total which tracks blended per-learner cost.

nodes.description: Total dollar spend on training programmes. Used as ROI denominator in cost+outcome pair analysis.

domain_context.lms: COLLIDER — driven simultaneously by assignment_pressure (overdue_count pathway) AND genuine engagement (session_to_user_ratio pathway). Using completion_rate as a filter introduces collider bias — creates spurious correlations between overdue_count and session behaviour. Always use compliance_rate as the terminal for attribution analysis. completion_rate is valid as an intermediate metric or for descriptive reporting only.

nodes.description: Percentage of assigned training items completed. COLLIDER — blocked as Shapley terminal. Use compliance_rate instead.

domain_context.lms: COLLIDER — driven simultaneously by ilt_waiting_list_count (demand pressure) AND no_show_rate (supply-side failure) AND plan_completion_pct (scheduling intent). Conditioning on ilt_attendance_rate introduces bias between demand and supply drivers. Use cert_overdue_count as terminal for ILT capacity chain analysis.

nodes.description: Percentage of scheduled ILT seats resulting in confirmed attendance. COLLIDER — influenced by multiple independent paths.

domain_context.lms: Primary mediator on the 28-day reachable path to compliance_rate. ϕ=0.58 for Shapley attribution in compliance gap analysis. Intervention target for overdue outreach campaigns. Driven by compliance_assigned_distribution (root) and lm_learner_total (headcount confounder).

nodes.description: Total count of assignments past their due date. Primary reachable mediator in 28-day compliance gap remediation window.

domain_context.lms: Second mediator in the overdue→compliance chain. Receives from overdue_count (7d lag) and feeds compliance_rate (7d further lag). Total chain: overdue → missed → compliance = 28 days. Boundary between recoverable overdue and recorded non-compliance event.

nodes.description: Count of training deadlines that passed without completion. Distinct from overdue_count — represents finalized non-compliance events.

domain_context.lms: Multi-session engagement depth per user per week. Driven by login_count_weekly_trend. Feeds completion_rate (collider) with 7d lag. High ratio distinguishes active learners from passive badge-collectors.

nodes.description: Average sessions per active learner per week. Engagement depth signal between login frequency and completion rate.

domain_context.lms: Accumulated count of non-compliance events within a fiscal year. Feeds audit evidence posture. Benchmark_analysis and YoY trend intents use this for historical trajectory.

nodes.description: Total non-compliance events in the year. Used for audit evidence and YoY benchmark analysis.

domain_context.lms: Mean assessment score across active learners. Mediates between self_directed_ratio, attempt_avg_per_learner, and plan_completion_pct upstream; pass_rate downstream with 14d lag.

nodes.description: Average raw score across all assessment attempts in the period.

domain_context.lms: Shape of the score distribution (bimodal, skewed, bell). A bimodal distribution signals two distinct learner populations — remediation-needed vs already-proficient. Feeds pass_rate with 14d lag.

nodes.description: Statistical distribution of assessment scores. Identifies remediation cohorts.

domain_context.lms: Mean number of assessment attempts before pass or abandon. High value with low pass_rate indicates content friction. High value with high pass_rate indicates acceptable persistence pattern.

nodes.description: Average attempts per learner per assessment. Distinguishes content friction from content difficulty.

domain_context.lms: Percentage completion of a specific named training plan's activities. Mediates into score_average, eval_completion_rate, ilt_attendance_rate, and cert_overdue_count. Used directly as the terminal in training_plan_dashboard intent.

nodes.description: Percentage of a training plan's assigned activities completed by the assigned cohort.

domain_context.lms: Rate at which learners complete post-training evaluations (level 1 reactions). Feeds pass_rate with same-day lag. Low rate degrades signal quality for content effectiveness decisions.

nodes.description: Percentage of learners who complete the post-training evaluation. Level 1 Kirkpatrick data quality indicator.

domain_context.lms: Absolute count of assessment failures. Driven by pass_rate (same-day, inverse). High fail_count in mandatory compliance modules directly delays compliance_rate improvement even when overdue_count is resolved.

nodes.description: Total count of assessment attempts resulting in a failing score.

domain_context.lms: Number of certifications assigned to employees requiring completion or renewal. Drives cert_expiring_soon with 30d lag. Demand-side root for the certification capacity chain.

nodes.description: Count of certification requirements assigned across the active workforce.

domain_context.lms: Count of certifications expiring within the alert window (typically 30–60 days). Drives ilt_waiting_list_count with 14d lag as learners rush to renew via ILT. Key early-warning metric for certification compliance rate degradation.

nodes.description: Count of employee certifications expiring within the alert window. Leading indicator for ILT demand spikes.

domain_context.lms: Count of expired or missed certification renewals. Direct driver of cert_compliance_rate (14d lag) and ce_compliance_rate (14d lag). Driven by ilt_attendance_rate (capacity constraint) and plan_completion_pct (scheduling intent).

nodes.description: Count of certification requirements past their renewal deadline.

domain_context.lms: Number of learners queued for ILT sessions that are at capacity. Driven by cert_expiring_soon (demand) and ilt_instructor_count (supply). High waitlist → high no_show_rate (frustration abandonment) AND reduced ilt_attendance_rate.

nodes.description: Number of learners on ILT session waitlists. Capacity pressure mediator.

domain_context.lms: Percentage of registered ILT seats resulting in no-show. Driven by ilt_waiting_list_count (waitlist frustration → cancellation without notice). Feeds both completion_count_total (−) and ilt_attendance_rate (−) with same-day lag. No-shows waste seat capacity and inflate cost.

nodes.description: Percentage of ILT registrations resulting in learner no-show. Drives capacity waste and cost inflation.

domain_context.lms: Average seat capacity across scheduled ILT sessions. Driven by ilt_for_approval_count and ilt_waiting_list_count. Constrains throughput for certification renewal — low capacity extends cert_overdue_count.

nodes.description: Mean learner capacity per scheduled ILT session. Throughput constraint for certification renewal pipeline.

domain_context.lms: Count of ILT sessions in the approval queue. High count indicates admin bottleneck constraining capacity growth. Drives ilt_avg_capacity with 14d lag.

nodes.description: Count of ILT sessions awaiting scheduling approval. Administrative capacity bottleneck indicator.

domain_context.lms: Total training expenditure aggregated across all delivery modalities in the period. Driven by vendor_cost_per_hour and cancellation_count_total. Feeds training_cost_avg_per_learner with 14d lag. Distinct from training_spend_total (budget allocation vs actual expenditure).

nodes.description: Total training cost across all modalities in the period. Intermediate cost aggregation node.

domain_context.lms: Count of ILT session registrations cancelled by learner or admin. Each cancellation has a cost impact (lost seat revenue + admin overhead). Feeds training_cost_total as a waste driver. High cancellation rate often co-occurs with high no_show_rate.

nodes.description: Total ILT registrations cancelled in the period. Cost waste driver.

domain_context.lms: Absolute count of completed training activities. Denominator for training_cost_avg_per_learner and training_cost_completed. Decreases when no_show_rate is high. Used in YoY trend analysis.

nodes.description: Absolute count of training completions in the period. Cost efficiency denominator.

domain_context.lms: Portion of total training cost attributable to completed training only (excludes no-shows, cancellations). Used to compute effective cost per successful completion.

nodes.description: Training cost for sessions resulting in a completion — excludes wasted spend.

domain_context.lms: Top-of-funnel count for a specific programme or curriculum. Entry point for funnel_analysis — 'assigned' is stage 1 of the assign→start→complete→certify funnel.

nodes.description: Count of learners at the top of the training funnel — assigned but not yet started.

domain_context.lms: Count of learners who have launched at least one activity in a programme. Stage 2 of the funnel. Drop from assigned to started reveals friction at the awareness or motivation layer.

nodes.description: Count of learners who have launched (but not completed) at least one activity.

domain_context.lms: Conversion loss rate at a specific stage in the funnel. A high stage_drop_rate at 'started→completed' indicates content friction or length issues. A high drop at 'assigned→started' indicates awareness or motivation failures.

nodes.description: Percentage of learners who do not advance from one funnel stage to the next.

domain_context.lms: Bottom-of-funnel count — learners who reached full certification from the assigned cohort. Terminal for funnel_analysis intent. Conversion from funnel_assigned_count to funnel_certified_count is the overall funnel effectiveness metric.

nodes.description: Count of learners reaching certification from the assigned funnel cohort. Funnel analysis terminal.

domain_context.lms: Count of skills defined as required for a role family. Root node — set externally by job architecture or framework mapping. Changes to this count directly change role_skill_match_rate without any training activity changing.

nodes.description: Count of skills defined as required for each role family. External root — set by job architecture.

domain_context.lms: Count of curricula (multi-course programmes) completed by a learner or cohort. Maps to skill coverage — one curriculum completion typically evidences multiple skills. Mediates required_skill_count → role_skill_match_rate.

nodes.description: Count of multi-course curricula fully completed. Maps to skill coverage evidence.

domain_context.lms: Average minutes per learning session. Longer sessions correlate with higher completion rates and better assessment scores. Low session duration with high frequency may indicate check-in without real engagement.

nodes.description: Average minutes spent per learning session. Quality-of-engagement signal.

domain_context.lms: Percentage of nudge/reminder communications resulting in a learner login within 48 hours. Low response rate when login_count_weekly_trend is stable rules out nudge campaigns as effective intervention — suggests content or assignment-load issues instead.

nodes.description: Fraction of reminder messages leading to a learner action within 48 hours.

domain_context.lms: Count of second or subsequent assessment attempts. High repeat_attempt_count with a pass on the retry indicates learner persistence rather than content failure. Used in behavioral_analysis to separate motivated vs disengaged learner cohorts.

nodes.description: Count of assessment re-attempts (attempt_number > 1). Persistence vs friction differentiator.

domain_context.lms: Year-over-year change in completion rate. Used in benchmark_analysis and YoY activity trend charts. Positive delta with declining engagement metrics is a leading warning of unsustainable completion through administrative pressure rather than genuine learning.

nodes.description: Percentage point change in completion rate vs the same period in the prior year.
